100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 11, 1926, the British government faced mounting pressure as coal miners continued their strike, challenging Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin's administration amid fears of widespread unrest. In the United States, a significant tornado swept through parts of Texas, causing destruction and loss of life, while in Germany, the political climate remained tense with ongoing reparations discussions following the Great War. Meanwhile, advancements in aviation were highlighted as Charles Lindbergh prepared for his historic transatlantic flight, capturing the public's imagination.
Updated: Saturday, April 11, 2026 at 07:26:24 AM CDT
